title: Understanding the Density Function
text: The density function is given by $$rho(x,y)=\rho_0 x y$$. This means that the density varies with both $$x$$ and $$y$$ coordinates. How does this affect the calculation of the center of mass?

title: Formula for Center of Mass
text: The y-coordinate of the center of mass is given by the formula: $$y_{cm}$$ $$=$$ $$\frac{1}{M}$$ * $$\int_{0}^{b} y*rho(x,y) \,dy$$ * $$\int_{0}^{a} dx \,dx$$. What does each part of this formula represent?

title: Calculate the Total Mass
text: To find the total mass M, integrate the density function over the entire area of the rectangle: M $$=$$ $$\int_{0}^{a} rho(x,y) \,dx$$ * $$\int_{0}^{b} dy \,dy$$.

title: Integrate the Density Function
text: Calculate the integral of the density function over the y-axis: $$\int_{0}^{b} y*rho(x,y) \,dy$$.

title: Integrate Over the x-axis
text: Now, integrate the result from the previous step over the x-axis: $$\int_{0}^{a} \frac{\rho_0 x b^2}{2} \,dx$$.

title: Calculate the Total Mass
text: Integrate the density function over the entire area to find the total mass M: $$\int_{0}^{a} \rho_0 x y \,dx$$ * $$\int_{0}^{b} dy \,dy$$.

title: Find the y-coordinate of the Center of Mass
text: Use the formula for $$y_{cm}$$ and the results from previous steps to find the y-coordinate of the center of mass.
